steps.title: Use the rating definition
steps.content: The conclusion is about which study better explained the methodology. The passage defines traceability as clarity in describing procedures, so traceability is the relevant rating.

steps.title: Challenge traceability
steps.content: Choice B supports the conclusion. Choice C is about category consistency, not how well the methodology was explained. Choice D gives only Richter-Klein's ratings without comparing Vogel's traceability. A lower traceability rating for Vogel would directly challenge the conclusion. The answer is A.
